fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Alaska Audiologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a audiologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Alaska - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: audiologist license reciprocity alaska, how to transfer audiologist license to alaska, audiologist compact alaska, alaska audiologist endorsement application

board.name: Board of Examiners in Audiology and Speech-Language Pathology
board.parent_dept: Alaska Department of Commerce, Community, and Economic Development
board.mailing_address:
State of Alaska
Department of Commerce, Community, and Economic Development
Division of Corporations, Business and Professional Licensing
PO Box 110806
Juneau, AK 99811-0806

quick_facts.exam_name: Audiology Examination administered by the Praxis Examination Program

license_types.title: AuD, CCC-A
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a audiologist in Alaska.

steps.title: Obtain Required Degree
steps.description: Complete a master's or doctoral degree in audiology from an accredited educational institution.

steps.title: Pass Praxis Examination
steps.description: Successfully pass the Praxis II in Audiology examination, administered by ETS.

steps.title: Complete Supervised Professional Experience
steps.description: Complete at least nine months of full-time (or 18 months part-time) supervised professional experience, as outlined in Alaska regulations. This may involve obtaining a temporary license prior to applying for full licensure.

steps.title: Create Account and Apply Online
steps.description: Navigate to the MyLicense Online Services portal, register for an account if you don't have one, log in, and begin a new Audiologist license application.

steps.title: Submit Application and Documentation
steps.description: Complete the online application form, upload all required supporting documents, including official transcripts, Praxis scores, and documentation of supervised professional experience, and pay the application and initial license fees through the MyLicense portal.

steps.title: Board Review and Licensure
steps.description: The Board of Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology will review your complete application and credentials. Upon approval, your Alaska Audiologist license will be issued.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the Praxis. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Audiologists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Alaska audiologist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Alaska have reciprocity for audiologists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers audiologist practice in Alaska. To work in Alaska, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my audiologist license to Alaska?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Alaska while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Audiologists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Audiologist in Alaska
hero.dek: Audiologists seeking initial licensure or licensure by endorsement in Alaska must apply through the MyLicense Online Services portal managed by the Division of Corporations, Business and Professional Licensing.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps: Navigate to the MyLicense Online Services portal, select 'Apply for a License', and then 'Register for an Account' to create a user ID and password. Once registered, log in and select 'New Application' to begin the Audiologist licensure process.
